data.content:
Here are the three MSBuild evaluation performance issues in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Import Chain (`level1 → level2 → level3`)

The `.csproj` imports `level1.props`, which imports `level2.props`, which imports `level3.props`. During the **evaluation phase**, MSBuild must resolve and parse each import sequentially before it can finish evaluating properties. Deep/nested import chains increase evaluation time linearly—each level adds file I/O, XML parsing, and property/item merging overhead. In larger projects these chains can be much deeper and become a real bottleneck.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```

The `**\*.*` glob forces MSBuild to recursively enumerate **every file** in the project directory tree during evaluation. Only `bin` and `obj` are excluded—meaning directories like `.git/`, `node_modules/`, or any other large subtree are fully scanned. This is purely evaluation-phase work (item expansion), so it slows every design-time build and IDE load.

**Fix:** Restrict the glob to specific extensions/folders, or add more exclusions via `DefaultItemExcludes`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DefaultItemExcludes>$(DefaultItemExcludes);node_modules\**;.git\**</DefaultItemExcludes>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### 3. File I/O via Property Function During Evaluation

```xml
<BuildNotes Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```

`[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText(...)` is a **property function** that performs synchronous file I/O during the evaluation phase. Evaluation should be fast, declarative property/item resolution. Disk reads here block the evaluator and run on every evaluation (IDE refreshes, `dotnet build`, restores, etc.). This work belongs in a **target** (execution phase), where it only runs when actually needed:

```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt" Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" PropertyName="BuildNotes" />
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

---

### Evaluation vs. Execution Phase (Key Concept)

MSBuild processes a project in two phases:
- **Evaluation** — parses XML, resolves imports, expands properties/items, evaluates globs. Runs on *every* load (IDE, restore, build). Must be fast.
- **Execution** — runs targets/tasks. Only happens during an actual build.

All three issues above add unnecessary work to the evaluation phase, slowing every interaction with the project.
